briefs.title: Council to vote on North Road BIA renewal and e-bike bylaw changes in Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on several items at its April 14, 2026 meeting, including whether to renew the North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) — a special zone where local businesses collectively fund area improvements — and proposed bylaw changes that would allow e-bike share and electric kick scooter programs on city streets and in parks.
briefs.action_required: If you operate on North Road, check the BIA renewal outcome — it directly affects your business levy.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — North Road BIA renewal and e-bike bylaw vote

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby March 24 Meeting Recap — Zoning, Housing, and Fee Bylaws
briefs.summary: At its March 24, 2026 meeting, Burnaby City Council voted on several items that are now moving forward for further review or future approval — including rezoning applications for mixed-use and self-storage developments, a $4.2M water metering contract, updates to parking meter and fee bylaws, and endorsement of a new Poverty Reduction Strategy. Some items, like the modular housing pilot project, were sent back to staff for more review before a final decision is made.
briefs.action_required: Review the rezoning and bylaw changes relevant to your property or business and watch for upcoming public hearings.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Amendment Bylaw No. 2, 2026 — final adoption
Burnaby Parking Meter and EV Charging Meter Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026 — final adoption
Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Amendment Bylaw No. 2, 2026 — final adoption

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Transportation & Logistics

briefs.title: Council to Consider Letter Urging Federal Action on Chlorine Rail Transport Safety
briefs.summary: At a special meeting on April 1, 2026, Burnaby Council voted unanimously to have Mayor Hurley write a letter to Canada's Minister of Transport raising serious safety concerns about liquid chlorine being transported by rail through densely populated areas of the Lower Mainland. The concern stems from a nearby chlorine plant in North Vancouver that ships an average of four rail cars of liquid chlorine through Burnaby every day — a risk that presenters say has not been fully assessed.
briefs.action_required: If your business is near Burnaby rail corridors, monitor council updates on chlorine transport safety measures.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Day of Mourning Proclamation for Workers Killed or Injured on the Job
briefs.summary: Council will consider a request from the New Westminster & District Labour Council to officially declare April 28th as the 'Day of Mourning for Workers Killed and Injured on the Job.' The group is also asking Council to observe a minute of silence at the April 14, 2026 meeting and to encourage city staff to do the same at 11:00 a.m. on April 28th.
briefs.action_required: If workplace safety matters to your business, attend the April 14, 2026 Council meeting to hear the presentation.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — Day of Mourning delegation presentation
Day of Mourning for Workers — minute of silence requested by April 28

briefs.industries: Labour & Employment, Construction & Development, Professional Services

briefs.title: Council to vote on North Road BIA renewal, e-bike rules, and housing updates
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on several items at its April 14, 2026 meeting. Key items include whether to proceed with renewing the North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) — a special zone where local businesses collectively fund area improvements — plus proposed bylaw changes that would set rules for e-bike share and electric kick scooter programs on Burnaby streets and in parks.
briefs.action_required: If your business is in the North Road BIA, check the petition results — enough objections were received to potentially block the renewal.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — vote on North Road BIA renewal and e-bike bylaw changes

briefs.title: Council to vote on appointing new Chief Election Officer for Burnaby's 2026 municipal election
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on appointing Dennis Back as Chief Election Officer for the 2026 City of Burnaby General Local Election, scheduled for October 17, 2026. The original officer appointed in September 2025 is on leave and is not expected to return in time, so a replacement is needed to keep election planning on track.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on April 14, 2026 — this affects who runs Burnaby's next election.

key_dates.label: Burnaby 2026 General Local Election day

briefs.title: Council to Consider: North Road BIA Renewal Blocked by Sufficient Owner Opposition
briefs.summary: Council is set to receive a report showing that enough property owners opposed the proposed 5-year renewal of the Burnaby North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) — a special tax zone where businesses pay a shared levy to fund local improvements. Because 55% of affected property owners (representing 52% of total assessed value) signed petitions against the renewal, staff are advising Council that it cannot move forward with adopting the renewal bylaw.
briefs.action_required: If you support the BIA renewal, contact the North Road Business Improvement Association about next steps.
briefs.industries: Retail, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Professional Services

briefs.title: Council to vote on annual housekeeping decisions for Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on routine annual corporate maintenance matters for the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation (BHA) — the City-owned company created to develop and manage affordable housing. If approved, Council would skip a formal annual general meeting (AGM) in favour of written resolutions, appoint KPMG as auditor, confirm December 12, 2026 as the official reference date, receive the 2025 financial statements, and formally appoint 10 directors to the BHA board for either two- or three-year terms.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council decision — this affects how Burnaby's affordable housing corporation is governed in 2026.

key_dates.label: BHA annual reference date — Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation 2026

briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Construction & Development, Finance & Insurance

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Burnaby Housing Authority 2025 Financials and $2M Operating Budget
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to receive the Burnaby Housing Authority's (BHA) first-ever audited financial statements and its 2026 operating budget. Accepting these documents is the final condition that must be met before the City can release $2 million in operating funding to the BHA for 2026 — money that will keep the BHA running as it advances two new rental housing projects in Burnaby.
briefs.action_required: Watch the April 14, 2026 council meeting — this decision affects housing supply and public funds in Burnaby.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — BHA financials and 2026 budget up for receipt
BHA $25M GIC investment matures — Burnaby Housing Authority

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Finance & Insurance

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on bylaws for e-bike share and e-scooter programs
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on amendments to three Burnaby bylaws that would set the rules for a new shared e-bike rental program and for privately owned electric kick scooters on city streets and in parks. A proposed bylaw would define where these devices can be ridden and parked, allow the city to create designated parking zones, and set fines for riding in areas not approved for these devices.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — new rules could affect where you ride or park e-bikes and e-scooters near your business.

key_dates.label: Burnaby e-bike share and e-scooter bylaw amendments — council vote

briefs.title: Council to vote on submitting Burnaby's first housing target progress report to BC
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on authorizing staff to send Burnaby's first six-month progress report to the BC Ministry of Housing and Municipal Affairs. The report covers September 2025 to February 2026 and shows roughly 910 new homes were completed — putting Burnaby on track for Year 1 of its provincially mandated target of 10,240 new homes by 2030, though slowing construction and a pause in provincial affordable housing funding are raising concerns about future progress.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — it may signal changes to housing supply and development timelines in Burnaby.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Year 1b Housing Target Progress Report — BC submission deadline

briefs.title: Council to vote on: Burnaby continuing Building Benchmark BC energy tracking program
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on whether to continue the city's participation in Building Benchmark BC (BBBC) for Year 7 in 2026. This voluntary program helps building owners track and compare their energy use and carbon emissions — at no extra cost to the city, using $16,500 already set aside in the existing budget.
briefs.action_required: If you own a building in Burnaby, watch for BBBC recruitment outreach — joining is free and gives you energy data.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — Building Benchmark BC Year 7 participation approval

briefs.title: Council to vote on granting FortisBC a right-of-way for natural gas work at Burnaby Mountain
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on giving FortisBC Energy Inc. a Statutory Right-of-Way — a legal permission to access and use a strip of city-owned land — over parts of two properties near Burnaby Mountain Conservation Area. This would allow FortisBC to install, operate, and maintain natural gas infrastructure needed to service the Mintara restaurant and an existing gas main that has been running without proper legal access since the 1960s.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the April 14, 2026 council decision if your business operates near Burnaby Mountain or relies on FortisBC gas service in that area.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — FortisBC Burnaby Mountain right-of-way approval

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Natural Resources, Food Service

briefs.title: Council to vote on $1.35M contract for modular four-plex housing pilot in Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on awarding a $1,354,463 contract to Echelon Creative Inc. to build a modular (factory-built) four-unit residential building at 8315 14th Avenue as a pilot project. The build is part of a broader push to test low-carbon, factory-built housing as a faster and more affordable way to add rental homes in lower-density neighbourhoods.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the April 14, 2026 council decision — this pilot could shape future modular housing contracts in Burnaby.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — modular four-plex contract award decision

briefs.title: Council to vote on $2.4M contract for Jerry Rogers Creek culvert replacement in Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding a $2,404,076 contract to HANNA Infrastructure Ltd. to replace an aging, undersized culvert (a pipe or tunnel that carries water under a road or structure) at Jerry Rogers Creek. The new box culvert would be built to handle heavy rainfall expected once every 100 years, helping reduce flood risk in the area.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the April 14, 2026 council decision — construction near your business could affect access or utilities.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — Jerry Rogers Creek culvert contract award

briefs.title: Council to vote on $1.36M contract for Central Valley Greenway culvert and paving work
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding a $1,360,391 contract to Drake Excavating (2016) Ltd. to replace 16 culverts (underground drainage pipes) and complete final paving along the Central Valley Greenway trail between Sperling Avenue and Douglas Road. The project is already budgeted in the City's 2026–2030 Financial Plan, with funding split between City reserves and TransLink.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the April 14, 2026 council decision if your business operates near the Central Valley Greenway trail.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — Central Valley Greenway contract award public meeting

briefs.title: Council to vote on $450,000 contract extension with Rogers for city cellular services
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on approving a $450,000 contract change (an increase to an existing agreement) with Rogers Communications Canada Inc. to keep the city's cellular voice and data services running while the city switches to a new provider. This is a short-term, bridging arrangement — not a long-term deal — and a new contract with a different provider is expected to come before Council at a future meeting.
briefs.action_required: No direct action needed — this is an internal city contract and does not affect private businesses.

briefs.title: Council to vote on $579,600 contract extension for Burnaby city building HVAC services
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on extending its existing contract with Just Mechanical Ltd. to continue heating, ventilation, and air conditioning (HVAC) maintenance and repair at city-owned buildings. The proposed contract change is worth $579,600 including taxes and is meant as a short-term bridge while the city runs a new competitive bidding process to find a long-term service provider.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on April 14, 2026 — this affects HVAC service contracts with the City of Burnaby.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — HVAC contract change approval meeting

briefs.title: Council to vote on $394,942 Cisco WebEx Contact Center license renewal for Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on renewing a three-year software license contract with Long View Systems Corporation for approximately $394,942 (taxes included). The license keeps the City's phone-based customer service system running and supports IT staff support lines. If approved, the contract would run from May 5, 2026 to May 4, 2029.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the April 14, 2026 council decision — this is an internal city IT contract with no direct impact on most businesses.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Cisco WebEx Contact Center contract renewal start date

briefs.title: Council to vote on: $1.67M contract renewal for city fire protection services
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on renewing a contract with A.W. Fireguard Supplies (1991) Ltd. to inspect, maintain, and repair fire protection and emergency lighting systems at city-owned buildings. The proposed three-year renewal would run from July 1, 2026, to June 30, 2029, at an estimated cost of $1,666,061 including taxes.
briefs.action_required: If you supply fire protection services, watch this decision — it signals Burnaby's vendor for the next three years.

key_dates.label: Burnaby fire protection services contract renewal — council vote
Burnaby fire protection contract renewal period starts
Burnaby fire protection contract renewal period ends

briefs.title: Council to vote on external financial review and 20-storey Beresford Street rezoning
briefs.summary: At its April 14, 2026 meeting, Burnaby City Council is set to vote on two key committee items: hiring an outside reviewer to check the City's financial practices and reduce the risk of tax increases, and deciding whether to advance a proposed 20-storey apartment building at 5035 Beresford Street to its first formal readings. Neither decision has been made yet.
briefs.action_required: Watch the April 14 Council meeting — both votes could affect local taxes and development near your business.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — vote on external financial review and Beresford rezoning

briefs.industries: Finance & Insurance, Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt

briefs.title: Council to vote on hiring external reviewer to assess Burnaby's financial practices
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on hiring an outside expert to review how the City manages its finances, plans spending, and sets priorities for major projects. The goal is to protect the City's financial health and reduce the risk of significant tax hikes or higher service costs for businesses and residents.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the April 14, 2026 council decision — it could signal future changes to business taxes or city service costs.

key_dates.label: Burnaby external financial review — Council vote takes place

briefs.industries: Finance & Insurance, Professional Services, Construction & Development

briefs.title: Proposed: 20-Storey Rental Apartment at 5035 Beresford Street Up for Council Approval
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on whether to advance a rezoning application (REZ #25-11) that would allow a 20-storey, 215-unit rental apartment building at 5035 Beresford Street — a site currently occupied by a printing shop. If approved, the rezoning bylaw would move to First and Second Reading at a future Council meeting, with several conditions still to be met before final approval.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council vote — this rezoning could affect nearby businesses and the local rental housing supply.

key_dates.label: Burnaby REZ #25-11 rezoning bylaw — Council consideration at meeting

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant

briefs.title: Council to Consider North Road BIA Renewal and Economic Growth Task Force Update
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on several items at its April 14, 2026 meeting, including a proposed renewal of the North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) — a special zone where local businesses pool funds for area improvements — and an update on the Mayor's Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth and Resilience. Council will also consider a report that a developer has chosen to forfeit (give up) their security deposit rather than build a public amenity pier at a Burnaby riverfront site.
briefs.action_required: If your business is in the North Road BIA, watch the April 14 Council vote — the BIA renewal outcome directly affects your area levy.

key_dates.label: Burnaby City Council meeting — North Road BIA renewal vote and consent agenda items

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Progress Update on Burnaby's Economic Growth Task Force
briefs.summary: Council will consider an update on the Mayor's Task Force on Burnaby Economic Growth and Resilience, a group of business and community leaders working to build a stronger local economy. The Task Force has held five workshops so far and is on track to deliver formal recommendations to Council on June 23, 2026.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Task Force's final recommendations to Council on June 23, 2026 — they may affect local business support programs.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Economic Growth Task Force — final recommendations presented to Council

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Developer Forfeits $1.36M Instead of Building Public Pier
briefs.summary: Council is set to receive a report on Oxford Properties' decision to forfeit (give up) $1,363,500 in security money rather than build a planned public amenity pier on the Fraser River near Riverbend Business Park. The developer could not get the required provincial land approval to build over the water, so under a legal agreement already in place, the City will keep the funds and decide later how to spend them on public amenities.
briefs.action_required: Watch for Council's future decision on how the $1.36M will be spent on public amenities near your area.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — Amenity Pier forfeiture report received for information

briefs.title: Council to Consider Information Reports — Burnaby Meeting April 14, 2026
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will consider a set of information reports at its April 14, 2026 meeting. No body text or details were provided for this agenda item, so the specific topics and any potential impact on local businesses are not yet known.
briefs.action_required: Check the full April 14, 2026 Burnaby Council agenda for report details that may affect your business.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — information reports considered

briefs.title: Council to vote on North Road BIA renewal — but sufficient objections received
briefs.summary: At its April 14, 2026 meeting, Burnaby City Council is set to consider final adoption of Bylaw #14797, which would renew the North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) — a defined zone where businesses pay a levy to fund local improvements — for a 5-year term. However, the agenda notes that enough property owners have submitted formal petitions against the renewal to potentially block it, so the outcome is uncertain.
briefs.action_required: If your business is in the North Road BIA, attend the April 14 Council meeting to follow the vote on the renewal.

key_dates.label: Burnaby North Road BIA renewal bylaw — Council final adoption vote

briefs.title: Council to vote on: Burnaby North Road BIA renewal blocked by sufficient petitions
briefs.summary: At its April 14, 2026 meeting, Burnaby City Council is set to consider the final adoption of Bylaw #14797, which would renew the Burnaby North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) — a defined zone where businesses collectively fund local improvements — for a 5-year term. However, the agenda notes that enough business owners submitted formal petitions against the renewal to potentially stop it from moving forward.
briefs.action_required: If you operate on North Road, check whether the BIA renewal passed or was defeated at the April 14 meeting.

key_dates.label: Burnaby North Road BIA Renewal Bylaw #14797 — final adoption vote

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby North Road Business Improvement Area Bylaw — Council to vote on final adoption
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on final adoption of a bylaw that would officially create the Burnaby North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) — a defined zone where businesses pool funds for local improvements like street beautification, graffiti removal, and business promotion. If approved, the BIA would run for five years (April 2026–March 2031), with over $1.75 million in total funding paid out to the Burnaby North Road Business Association. Important note: enough property owners have filed formal objections (petitions against) that Council must carefully consider whether to proceed.
briefs.action_required: If you own property or a business on North Road, contact Burnaby Council now — enough objections were filed to trigger a review.

key_dates.label: Burnaby North Road BIA bylaw — Council final adoption vote
Burnaby North Road BIA — proposed start date if bylaw adopted
Burnaby North Road BIA — annual budget submission deadline (Association to City)
Burnaby North Road BIA — proposed end date

briefs.title: No Action Items: Burnaby Council Correspondence Package — April 14, 2026
briefs.summary: No items of correspondence were brought forward for Council to make a decision on at the April 14, 2026 meeting. All correspondence received was published in the Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions package for that date — for reference only, with no votes or approvals required.

briefs.title: Council to Consider North Road BIA Renewal and Multiple City Contracts at April 14 Meeting
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on several items at its April 14, 2026 meeting, including whether to renew the North Road Business Improvement Area (BIA) — a special zone where local businesses collectively fund area improvements — for another five years. Council will also consider contract awards, bylaw changes for e-bikes and electric kick scooters, and an external review of the City's financial practices.
briefs.action_required: If your business is in the North Road BIA, watch the April 14 Council vote — it directly affects your BIA fees.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — North Road BIA renewal vote and other items

briefs.title: Council to Announce 2026 Bursary Awards for Eight Burnaby Secondary School Students
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on granting bursary awards (one-time financial grants) to eight secondary school students across Burnaby schools. If approved, staff would notify the recipients and their school principals, with the awards announced publicly at the April 14, 2026 Open Council meeting.
